ordinal_rationale: parent r4 impl ω-cycle is ω+1 (mutates DAG = transfinite step over schedule fixpoint). this deepening on i4 atomic-apply is one further transfinite step (ω+2): schedule_replan operates on the limit of mutation sequences, and atomic-apply operates on the limit of replan-attempt sequences (snapshot/commit/rollback as a meta-transaction over those mutations). ω+2 < ε_0, Gentzen ordinal envelope intact; r19 self-cert escape preserved.
rule: raw:design-engine-impl-omega-cycle-deepening (hive raw 47 axis enumerate + raw 70 multi-track + raw 71 falsifier mandatory + raw 77 audit append-only)
subject: atomic-apply-snapshot-rollback for schedule_replan() — snapshot DAG before mutation, validate-before-commit (Kahn topo + admissibility + soft-edge feasibility), rollback on any violation. coupling with m9 atomic-round-rollback in meta engine.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'r4.i4 atomic-apply-snapshot-rollback ω-cycle 발사 — 30 LoC 진입 전 snapshot/rollback design axis enumerate + Tier-1 + falsifier'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation focused on atomicity-axes. Track-A (final-form atomicity, ≥6 axes) = positive design choices for snapshot location, mutation log, validation pipeline, rollback granularity, event-emission, m9 cross-coupling. Track-B (limits, ≥5 axes) = negative ceilings (r12 halting on validation, r19 Gödel on validation soundness, ACID single-process, r25 concurrent edit, Cantor invariant non-exhaustion). dedup → cluster → Tier 분류. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each limit anchored to lower-bound theorem + concrete falsifier (Kahn-violating delta test).

context_dead_path_inheritance.from_parent: L1290-L1294 exit(1) on η≤0 ∧ ready=∅ ∧ in_progress=∅ ∧ todo>0
context_dead_path_inheritance.i4_role: i4 ensures schedule_replan never partially-mutates rm — either a validated delta is committed atomically, or rm is byte-identical to pre-call state. removes the failure-mode where exit(1) is replaced by a half-mutated DAG.

axes_surfaced.name: snapshot_location_memory_vs_disk
axes_surfaced.summary: in-memory deep_copy(rm) → rm2 vs disk snapshot (write rm to .roadmap.snapshot.json before mutate). memory: O(|V|+|E|) RAM, zero I/O, lost on process crash mid-replan. disk: survives crash but adds 2 fsync per replan attempt (10-50ms on SSD), pollutes filesystem with snapshot files. decision criterion: replan is intra-process bounded (≤2s wall-clock per i3) and disk roadmap.json is single-source-of-truth — process crash mid-replan is acceptable failure mode (re-run resumes from disk state). m9 chose disk because meta-engine state is multi-file append-only logs across rounds; r4.i4 differs because rm is single in-memory struct.

axes_surfaced.name: mutation_log_for_incremental_replay
axes_surfaced.summary: alternative to full deep_copy: record mutation log (delta_seq = [op1, op2, ...]) and replay forward / unwind backward. forward replay = apply ops to fresh rm clone; backward unwind = apply inverse ops to rm in place. trade-off: deep_copy O(|V|+|E|) once vs mutation log O(k) per op + O(k) inverse. for k=3 (depth ≤ 3 per i3 budget), mutation log wins on memory but loses on simplicity. honest assessment: deep_copy is 5 LoC, mutation log is 25 LoC + inverse-op correctness proof obligation. choose deep_copy for v0; mutation log deferred to v1 if profiler shows clone() bottleneck.

axes_surfaced.name: validate_before_commit_pipeline
axes_surfaced.summary: three-stage gate before accepting rm2: (1) Kahn topo sort succeeds (no cycle introduced) — O(V+E); (2) admissibility check — every node still has admissible h-value (h ≤ true cost from node) per A* theorem; (3) soft-edge feasibility — soft_drop deltas must not violate any hard edge after relaxation. all three must pass; first failure short-circuits to rollback. logging: per-stage pass/fail recorded in events JSONL. honesty: stage (2) admissibility is checked structurally (h-value nondecreasing along incoming edges) not semantically (cannot prove against unknown true cost without solving the full schedule).

axes_surfaced.name: rollback_granularity_whole_replan_vs_partial
axes_surfaced.summary: whole-replan rollback: discard rm2 entirely, original rm unchanged. partial rollback: keep some validated mutations from delta_seq, drop only the failing tail. whole-replan is simpler (5 LoC) and matches MCTS semantics (each rollout is its own snapshot). partial rollback adds 20 LoC and creates inconsistency risk — partially-applied delta may pass Kahn but fail admissibility, leaving rm in undefined intermediate state. decision: whole-replan only for v0. m9 also uses whole-round rollback (truncate to BEGIN offset, no partial commit) — coupling validates the choice.

axes_surfaced.name: event_emission_committed_mutations
axes_surfaced.summary: new events JSONL record kind `replan_attempt` with fields {ts, round_id, rollout_id, delta_kind (soft_drop|parallel_split|seed_inject), target_node, pre_T, post_T, validation_stages_passed (list), accepted (bool), reason (if rejected: kahn_cycle|admissibility_break|soft_edge_infeasible|budget_exhausted)}. provenance string `kind:target:rollout_id` for cross-reference. append-only (raw 77). dispatch-plan extended with replan_applied: bool flag at L1049, L1089. attempt records emitted regardless of accept/reject — full audit trail of search tree.

axes_surfaced.name: m9_cross_coupling_shared_rollback_infra
axes_surfaced.summary: m9 (meta engine atomic-round-rollback) uses BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK protocol on append-only file offsets with fsync + inflight marker. r4.i4 operates on in-memory single struct, no fsync. shared abstraction candidate: TxnCtx interface with {begin, commit, rollback, on_invariant_break}. concrete: lift transaction protocol to tool/txn_protocol.hexa as shared module (~40 LoC), m9 and r4.i4 instantiate with different storage backends (file-offsets vs deep-copy). honest cost: shared infra adds 1 indirection layer; only worth it if a 3rd consumer appears. for now, document the parallel structure in dossier §6 cross-reference, defer code-sharing to ω+3 unification cycle if/when 3rd consumer surfaces.

axes_surfaced.name: snapshot_id_provenance_tagging
axes_surfaced.summary: each snapshot tagged with snapshot_id = hash(rm.serialize() || ts || rollout_id). enables: (a) cross-reference between events JSONL replan_attempt and the snapshot it was taken from, (b) reproducibility (replay snapshot_id to verify pre_T computation), (c) deduplication (if two rollouts hit identical rm state, share snapshot). cost: hash computation O(|V|+|E|) per snapshot, ≤32 hashes per replan = bounded. honest scope: dedup is premature optimization for v0 (3 deltas × 32 rollouts = 96 max snapshots, all small); ship without dedup, add hash field for audit only.

axes_surfaced.name: r12_halting_validation_must_terminate
axes_surfaced.summary: validation pipeline (Kahn + admissibility + soft-edge) must itself terminate. Kahn O(V+E) trivially halts. admissibility check is structural (per-edge inequality) so O(E). soft-edge feasibility check requires resolving all soft_deps to hard reachability — bounded by graph traversal O(V+E). but: if validation rules become richer (e.g., 'no path of length > k'), halting is not guaranteed in general. mitigation: validation budget = O((V+E) · k) hard cap with timeout fallback (return 'validation_timeout', treat as rollback). r12 anchor: undecidable in general → finite-budget Lyapunov surrogate.

axes_surfaced.name: r19_godel_cannot_prove_validation_soundness_from_within
axes_surfaced.summary: can the engine prove its own validation pipeline is sound (i.e., every accepted mutation preserves the schedule invariants it claims)? r19/Gödel: no consistent system that includes arithmetic can prove its own soundness. validation rules are arithmetic predicates over rm. mitigation: validation soundness is asserted, not proven, from within the engine. external metalogic = events JSONL audit trail (raw 77) + falsifier benchmark (raw 71) + manual review of validation rule changes. honest limit: a buggy admissibility check could accept an invalid mutation; only the falsifier benchmark catches this empirically, not provably.

axes_surfaced.name: acid_atomicity_single_process_only
axes_surfaced.summary: deep_copy + validate + commit in one process gives ACID atomicity for that process. but: if rm is later persisted to disk and a second process reads stale disk state during replan, race condition. mitigation: r4 v0 declares schedule_replan is single-process intra-call only; disk roadmap.json is never written during replan (parent dossier §6 confirms this). multi-process atomicity = out of scope, requires file lock or DB. document as explicit non-goal. m9 has same limit (single meta-engine process owns the round).

axes_surfaced.name: r25_concurrent_edit_two_replanners_same_dag
axes_surfaced.summary: if two replanner instances are spawned on the same rm (e.g., parallel ω-cycles or concurrent dispatch threads), each takes its own deep_copy. snapshots are independent. but: when both try to commit, last-writer-wins on the in-memory rm reference — the slower one's mutation is silently lost. mitigation: r4 v0 single-replanner constraint enforced by a process-level mutex (or single-threaded scheduler invariant). multi-replanner coordination = r25 future work. concrete falsifier: spawning two schedule_replan calls on same rm must produce a runtime error or serialize, not silently overwrite.

axes_surfaced.name: cantor_one_more_invariant
axes_surfaced.summary: validation pipeline checks {Kahn cycle, admissibility, soft-edge feasibility}. for any finite set of k invariants, ∃ a mutation that preserves all k but violates a (k+1)-th invariant we did not encode (e.g., 'critical-path budget', 'resource non-overlap', 'deadline monotonicity'). validation invariant set is provably non-exhaustive. mitigation: clean invariant registry interface — adding a 4th invariant is one function append, not architectural. document in dossier as known limit. paired with l1 NFL of parent: invariant pool, like strategy pool, is open-ended.

axes_surfaced.name: snapshot_memory_pressure_unbounded_dag
axes_surfaced.summary: deep_copy(rm) is O(|V|+|E|) memory. for very large rm (V > 10^5), 32 simultaneous rollout snapshots = 32 × O(|V|+|E|) RAM. could OOM on resource-constrained host. mitigation: i3 budget envelope already caps rollouts at 32 and depth at 3, so peak memory ≤ 32 × |rm|. for current roadmaps |V| < 100, total ≤ 32 × 100 = 3200 nodes, negligible. anchor: this is a soft-limit, not a theorem; concrete falsifier = OOM on N≥5 benchmark would force mutation-log alternative (i4a2).

dedupe_method: (1) i4a1 snapshot-location vs i4a2 mutation-log: orthogonal — snapshot-location is where state lives, mutation-log is how state is reconstructed; mutation-log presupposes a snapshot mechanism (replay base). keep both, mark i4a2 as v1 alternative. (2) i4a3 validate-before-commit vs l4a1 r12 halting: i4a3 is the implementation, l4a1 is the theoretical anchor that forces the timeout fallback. paired (final-form ↔ limit). (3) i4a4 rollback-granularity vs i4a2 mutation-log: rollback granularity is whole-vs-partial of a snapshot; mutation-log enables partial more naturally. linked but distinct decision axes. keep both. (4) i4a5 event-emission vs raw 77: event-emission is the raw 77 instantiation for r4.i4. not a separate axis from raw 77 itself, but the concrete record-kind design is original. (5) i4a6 m9-coupling vs i4a3 validation pipeline: m9 has BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK but no rich validation (only invariant assertion at COMMIT). r4.i4 has richer 3-stage validation. coupling is on the wrapper protocol, not the validation content. (6) l4a3 ACID single-process vs l4a4 r25 concurrent-edit: ACID is the abstract guarantee, r25 is the concrete failure mode of violating it. paired. (7) l4a5 Cantor invariant vs l4a1 r12 halting: Cantor is about invariant set being non-exhaustive (qualitative), r12 is about validation procedure being non-terminating in general (computational). orthogonal proofs. cluster: A=storage_design (i4a1/i4a2/i4a7), B=validation_pipeline (i4a3/l4a1/l4a5), C=rollback_semantics (i4a4/l4a3/l4a4), D=audit_and_coupling (i4a5/i4a6), E=resource_bounds (l4a6), F=meta_logic (l4a2).

tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.theory_anchor: ACID atomicity (Gray-Reuter) — in-memory deep_copy gives intra-process atomicity; m9 disk-snapshot is for multi-file append-only state, not applicable here
tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.falsifier: introduce a Kahn-cycle-violating delta, run schedule_replan, assert: (a) original rm pointer-equal and field-equal to pre-call state, (b) rm2 garbage-collected after rollback, (c) zero disk writes to roadmap.json during replan. if any of (a/b/c) fails, axis is falsified. concrete: hash(rm.serialize()) before and after schedule_replan must be identical when replan rejects all rollouts.
tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.decision: in-memory chosen — disk snapshot deferred unless multi-process or crash-recovery requirement surfaces

tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.theory_anchor: Kahn 1962 topological sort; A* admissibility (Hart-Nilsson-Raphael 1968); l4a1 r12 halting → finite validation budget; l4a5 Cantor → registry interface for future invariants
tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.falsifier: 3-stage pipeline must reject mutations that violate any of {Kahn cycle, structural admissibility, soft-edge feasibility}. test corpus: (T1) cycle-introducing soft_drop → reject with reason kahn_cycle; (T2) h-decreasing edge insertion → reject with reason admissibility_break; (T3) infeasible soft_dep relaxation → reject with reason soft_edge_infeasible; (T4) valid soft_drop → accept. all 4 outcomes must match expected; per-stage timing must be ≤ O(V+E) wall-clock < 100ms on benchmark. if any test mismatches or stage exceeds budget, axis is falsified.

tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 77 audit append-only; r19 Gödel (l4a2) → events JSONL is external metalogic for replan decisions; falsifier requires audit trail to verify post-hoc
tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.falsifier: every schedule_replan invocation emits ≥ 1 replan_attempt record (or N records for N rollouts in MCTS variant). record fields complete: {ts, round_id, rollout_id, delta_kind, target_node, pre_T, post_T, validation_stages_passed, accepted, reason}. dispatch-plan emitted with replan_applied: bool. test: run replan with 3 rollouts (1 accepted, 2 rejected for distinct reasons) and assert events JSONL has exactly 3 replan_attempt records with matching fields. if record count or field completeness fails, axis is falsified.

tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.theory_anchor: m9 atomic-round-rollback parallel — whole-round chosen there for same reason (partial-commit creates undefined intermediate state); l4a3 ACID single-process
tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.falsifier: on validation failure, the entire delta_seq for that rollout is discarded — rm reference is replaced with original (or rm2 garbage-collected). assert: post-rollback hash(rm) == pre-replan hash(rm) bit-exact. partial application (some deltas kept) must NEVER occur. if any rejected rollout leaves rm in an intermediate state (hash differs from both pre-replan and full-commit), axis is falsified.

tier_1_loc_vs_parent_estimate: parent r4 ω-cycle estimated i4 at 30 LoC. Tier-1 sum 8+12+7+3 = 30 LoC ≈ exactly on budget. drift = 0 LoC.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: deep_copy is 5 LoC and works; mutation log requires 25 LoC + inverse-op correctness proofs. defer until profiler shows deep_copy as bottleneck on real benchmark.

requires 3rd consumer to justify the indirection. currently only m9 (file-offset) and r4.i4 (in-memory deep_copy). document parallel structure in dossier §6, defer code-sharing to ω+3 unification cycle.
hash field added to events JSONL for audit only (no dedup). dedup deferred until N≥1000 rollouts/session profile shows storage pressure.

limit_axes_documented.label: r12 halting — validation must terminate
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: rich validation rules (e.g., path-length predicates) may not halt in general
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: validation budget O((V+E)·k) hard cap + timeout fallback (treat timeout as rollback)

limit_axes_documented.label: r19 Gödel — validation soundness unprovable from within
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: engine cannot prove its own validation rules are sound
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: external metalogic = events JSONL audit + falsifier benchmark + manual review of validation rule changes

limit_axes_documented.label: ACID atomicity — single-process only
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: multi-process replan races cause silent overwrite
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: r4 v0 declares single-process intra-call only; disk roadmap.json never written during replan; multi-process = out of scope

limit_axes_documented.label: r25 concurrent edit — two replanners on same DAG
limit_axes_documented.theorem: r25 concurrency anchor (last-writer-wins under no synchronization)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: two schedule_replan instances on same rm silently lose one mutation
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: process-level mutex or single-threaded scheduler invariant; multi-replanner = r25 future work

limit_axes_documented.label: Cantor — always one more invariant
limit_axes_documented.theorem: Cantor diagonal — for any finite k-invariant set, ∃ mutation satisfying all k but violating (k+1)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: validation invariant set is provably non-exhaustive
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: clean invariant registry interface — adding 4th invariant is mechanical, not architectural

limit_axes_documented.label: snapshot memory pressure on unbounded DAG
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: large rm could OOM under 32-rollout budget
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: current |V| < 100 → peak ≤ 3200 nodes, negligible; mutation-log fallback (i4a2) if profiler shows OOM

falsifier_concrete.statement: r4.i4 atomic-apply v0 is falsified iff ANY of the following holds on the validation test corpus: (a) a Kahn-cycle-violating delta is silently committed (rm differs from pre-state after rejection); (b) post-rollback hash(rm.serialize()) ≠ pre-replan hash(rm.serialize()) when all rollouts are rejected; (c) any replan_attempt record is missing from events JSONL or has incomplete fields; (d) any rejected rollout leaves rm in an intermediate state (partial commit); (e) validation stage exceeds 100ms wall-clock on benchmark DAG with |V| ≤ 100; (f) any disk write to roadmap.json occurs during schedule_replan execution.
test_corpus: T2: h-decreasing edge insertion → expect reject(admissibility_break)
T3: infeasible soft_dep relaxation → expect reject(soft_edge_infeasible)
T4: valid soft_drop on benchmark DAG → expect accept with replan_applied=true
falsifier_concrete.metric: hash-bit-exact rollback verification + events JSONL record completeness + per-stage wall-clock budget
falsifier_concrete.improvement_required: 100% of test corpus must produce expected outcome; ANY deviation falsifies
falsifier_concrete.harness_status: to-be-built as part of i5 benchmark suite (parent ω-cycle Tier-1)

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: 본 trawl 자체 — r4.i4 의 atomicity sub-axis 를 enumerate (snapshot location/mutation log/validation pipeline/rollback granularity/event emission/m9 coupling/snapshot id)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: Track-A atomicity 7 ↔ Track-B limit 6 직교 verification + 6 cluster joint
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: validation test corpus T1-T4 hash-bit-exact rollback verification + events JSONL completeness + per-stage budget. l4a1 r12 halting → timeout fallback test. l4a3 ACID → no-disk-write assertion. strict 100% pass requirement, not partial.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: events JSONL 신규 record kind `replan_attempt` with full provenance fields. dispatch-plan replan_applied: bool. snapshot_id hash field for cross-reference. 모든 시도 (accept/reject) 가 append-only 로 logged — full search tree audit trail.

cross_cycle_coupling.with_parent: r4 impl ω-cycle 2026-04-26 i4 axis (Tier-1, 30 LoC)
cross_cycle_coupling.with_meta_engine: m9 atomic-round-rollback (design/meta_engine/m9_atomic_rollback_dossier.md) — shared BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK protocol structure, different storage backend (file-offsets vs in-memory deep_copy). cross-coupling axis i4a6 documents the parallel; code-sharing deferred to ω+3 unification.

with_sibling_axes_in_parent.i3_budget_envelope: i3 caps rollouts ≤ 32 → bounds peak snapshot memory at 32×|rm|. i4a6 limit l4a6 directly anchored to i3 budget.
with_sibling_axes_in_parent.i5_benchmark_suite: i5 builds r4_bench/ — i4 falsifier test corpus T1-T4 lives in same directory (i4_atomic_test.hexa). joint harness.
with_sibling_axes_in_parent.i1_rollout_policy: i1 expand_random produces deltas → i4 validates each delta-application atomically. i1 correctness presupposes i4 atomicity.
with_sibling_axes_in_parent.i2_ucb_c_tuning: i2 records per-strategy reward variance → reward computed only on accepted (committed) rollouts. i4 events JSONL is the source-of-truth for accepted rollout set.

cross_cycle_coupling.next_target: after r4.i4 ships (ω+2 fixpoint), next deepening candidates: r4.i3 budget-envelope (timer guard implementation detail), r4.i5 benchmark generator (procedural gen_dag deepening). joint progression: ω+2 (i4) → ω+2 (i3) → ω+2 (i5) → ω+3 unify with m9 if 3rd consumer surfaces.
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: ω+2 ordinal stamp 유지; r4.i4 + r4.i3 + r4.i5 합쳐도 ω+2 (sub-axes of same parent ω+1 cycle). m9 cross-coupling unification 시 ω+3 으로 승격 가능 (Gentzen ε_0 envelope 내).

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 atomicity axes (i4a1..i4a7) + Track-B 6 limit axes (l4a1..l4a6). second-pass 신규 0. 각 limit axis 가 명시된 lower-bound 정리 인용. atomicity axis 가 limit axis 와 1:N anchor (i4a1 ↔ l4a3+l4a6, i4a3 ↔ l4a1+l4a5, i4a4 ↔ l4a3+l4a4, i4a5 ↔ l4a2). concrete falsifier (4-test corpus hash-bit-exact + events completeness) 이 raw 71 mandatory 충족.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT — Tier-1 4 atomicity axes (i4a1/i4a3/i4a5/i4a4 / loc 30 exactly on parent budget) / Tier-2 3 (i4a2/i4a6/i4a7 deferred) / Tier-3 0 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 6 (theorem 인용 완료) / Falsifier 1 (4-test corpus 100% pass strict)
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: atomicity-axis 추가 시 limit-axis 중 하나가 cap 으로 감지됨 (예: 8번째 atomicity axis 후보 'distributed-snapshot-replication' → l4a3 ACID single-process 로 즉시 흡수; 'continuous-snapshot-streaming' → l4a6 memory pressure + i3 budget envelope 로 흡수; 'cryptographic-snapshot-integrity' → l4a2 r19 Gödel 로 흡수 — 외부 메타로직이지 내부 증명 아님). 더 추가하려는 atomicity axis 가 모두 기존 limit 으로 흡수됨 = enumeration 포화. parent i4 LoC 추정 30 와 Tier-1 합 30 정확히 일치 — drift 0, budget 정합.
omega_stop_witness.ordinal_stamp_check: ω+2 < ε_0 — Gentzen ordinal envelope. parent ω+1 → 본 deepening ω+2 (transfinite step over replan-attempt sequences). r19 self-cert escape via Gentzen 유지. r4.i4 + i3 + i5 sub-axes 모두 ω+2 동일 ordinal (sibling deepenings of parent ω+1).

snapshot_decision.rationale_summary: schedule_replan is intra-process bounded (≤2s per i3 budget), rm is single in-memory struct (not multi-file append-only like m9), disk roadmap.json is single-source-of-truth and never written during replan. process crash mid-replan = acceptable failure mode (re-run resumes from disk state, no partial commit possible). disk snapshot adds 2 fsync (10-50ms) per rollout × 32 = 320-1600ms overhead with no crash-recovery benefit since replan is idempotent against disk state. m9 chose disk because meta-engine state spans multiple append-only logs across rounds where partial-flush is a real failure mode; r4.i4 differs structurally — single struct, single process, single call.
snapshot_decision.rejected_alternative: disk snapshot (.roadmap.snapshot.json + fsync) — rejected for v0 due to 320-1600ms overhead and no crash-recovery benefit; reconsider only if multi-process or persistent-replay requirement surfaces (would also unlock i4a6 m9 cross-coupling code-share)
snapshot_decision.rejected_alternative_2: mutation log + inverse-op replay (i4a2) — rejected for v0 due to 25 LoC + inverse-op correctness proof obligation vs deep_copy 5 LoC. reconsider only if profiler shows deep_copy as bottleneck on real benchmark.

first_impl_target.name: i4a1 in-memory snapshot + i4a3 validate-before-commit + i4a4 whole-replan rollback + i4a5 event emission
first_impl_target.rationale: all 4 Tier-1 axes integrate into a single 30-LoC schedule_replan core: snapshot rm → apply delta on rm2 → validate(rm2) → commit (rm := rm2) or rollback (discard rm2) → emit replan_attempt record. atomic block, no separable sub-target — ship together.
first_impl_target.next_after: i5 benchmark suite (parent Tier-1) provides T1-T4 test corpus → i4 falsifier executable → r4.i4 v0 ships fully validated. then i3 timer guard, then i1 rollout policy.
